original: St Marcellinus begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Roman usurper Nepotianus, of the Constantinian dynasty, is defeated and killed by troops of the usurper Magnentius, in Rome
Fifth Council of Toledo, of 22 bishops, convened by Visigothic King Chintila to help shore up his reign
Otto I the Great gives away bishopric of Utrecht "foreestrecht"
Jews are expelled from Berne, Switzerland
Arnold II of Horne chosen bishop of Utrecht
Foundation stone laid for Ulm Minster, in Free Imperial City of Ulm, Holy Roman Empire, construction is not completed until 1890, when it becomes the tallest church in the world
Denmark, Norway and Sweden sign Union of Kalmar under Queen Margaretha
Battle of Arbedo between the Duke of Milan and the Swiss cantons
Burgundian army occupies Utrecht
English and Dutch fleet begin attack of the Spain fleet moored at Cádiz during the Anglo-Spanish war. Leads also to the looting and burning of the city of Cádiz.
Annales Ecclesiastici (Scientific History of Catholicism) published
Battle of Adwalton Moor: Royalists beat parliamentary army during First English Civil war
French premier Cardinal Mazarin calls Saint Louis Chamber together
The Deluge: Khmelnytsky Uprising - the Battle of Beresteczko ends with a Polish victory
The number of deaths in London from the Great Plague during June is recorded as 6,137 people
First trial for piracy by the British Admiralty Court outside of Britain finds Captain John Quelch and five crew guilty, sentenced to hang the same day
Russian army occupies Danzig
Pope Benedict XIV encyclical forbidding traffic in alms
Philippines close all non-catholic Chinese restaurants
Seven Years' War: The Battle of Domstadtl results in Austrian victory over Prussia in Moravia
Seige of Fort Recovery, Ohio, Anglo American army holds off an attack on the fort by an Indian confederacy
Glasgow Police Act establishes the City of Glasgow Police, considered the 1st modern-style municipal police force, in Glasgow, Scotland
Michigan Territory organizes
US naval hero Stephen Decatur ends attacks by Algerian pirates
Congress creates Indian Territory, present day Oklahoma, as a place for native peoples
Battle of Viervoet: Basotho forces led by Moshoeshoe defeat the British over the British imposed Walden line
French acrobat Charles Blondin is 1st to cross Niagara Falls on a tightrope
CSS Sumter slips past USS Brooklyn blockade
Battle of Glendale [Frayser's Farm], day 6 of 7 days battles, Virginia Confederate assault attack (US Civil War)
Battles in Hanover, Pennsylvania: 80 casualties
Dutch colony Suriname counts population of 33,000 slaves
Skirmish at Sporting Hill, Pennsylvania
8 alleged conspirators in assassination of Lincoln are found guilty
Ada Kepley becomes 1st American female law college graduate (Old University of Chicago, later Northwestern)
Guatemala revolts for agrarian reforms
Serbia declares war on Turkey
Ex-khedive Ismael Pasha leaves Cairo with train full of stolen goods
Henry Highland Garnet named US minister to Liberia
Excelsior diamond (blue-white 995 carats) then world's largest, discovered in Jagersfontein Mine, South Africa
Korea declares independence from China, asks for Japanese aid
London's Tower Bridge opens across the Thames
William S. Hadaway patents an electric stove in the US
Winton Motor Carriage Company publishes the first known automobile ad in Scientific American using the headline “dispense with a horse.”
Spin bowler Jack Hearne takes England cricket's first Test hat-trick vs Australia in drawn 3rd Test at Headingley; dismisses champion middle order trio Clem Hill, Syd Gregory and Monty Noble
4 German liners burn at Hobokon Docks in New Jersey, 326 die
Hoboken Docks fire in and around the Hoboken, New Jersey piers of the Norddeutscher Lloyd shipping company on the Hudson River kills at least 326 people
Cleveland is 1st AL team to hit 3 consecutive HRs in same inning
The crew of the Russian battleship "Georgei Pobiedonosets" mutinies in support of the "Potemkin", which mutinied three days earlier
The mutinous battleship Potemkin arrives in Odessa in the Russian Empire, where sailors take the bodies of dead crewmen ashore and join civilians in revolutionary actions during the 1905 Revolution
John Hope becomes 1st black president of Atlanta Baptist College (later known as Morehouse College)

MLB Pittsburgh Pirates host the first game at Forbes Field, losing 3-2 to the Chicago Cubs in front of 30,338 fans in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania: club owner Barney Dreyfuss names the first steel and concrete stadium after British general John Forbes, who fought in the French and Indian War and named the city
Adolphe Messimy appointed French Minister of War
US Assay Office in St Louis, Missouri closes
To increase the peacetime strength of the German Army, the Reichstag pass the Army and Finance Bills, a massive defense buildup
Prominent US Socialist and Pacifist Eugene Debs is arrested on charges of denouncing the government, a violation of the Espionage Act of 1917
The South African Reserve Bank is established
England score 2-503 in day's play v South Africa at Lord's
Charles Jenkins is granted the U.S. patent for Transmitting Pictures over Wireless (early television)
Nicaraguan revolutionary Augusto César Sandino issues his Manifesto Politico
US Assay Office in Deadwood, South Dakota closes
Radio Service Bulletin lists radio stations call signs that are to be changed to conform with international standards
1st round-the-world radio broadcast Schenectady, NY
50,000 demonstrate in Antwerp against fascism/war
US Assay Offices close in Helena, MT, Boise, ID and Salt Lake City, UT
Detroit radio executive George Richards pays $7,952.08 to buy NFL's Portsmouth Spartans; moves team to Detroit and rebrands it to 'Lions'
French Equatorial Africa reconstituted into a single French colony and administrative unit
Danno O'Mahoney beats Ed George in Boston, to become wrestling champ
The Senegalese Socialist Party holds its first congress
40 hour work week law approved for US federal employees
The world's first emergency call telephone service is launched in London using the number 999
Final game at Philadelphia's Baker Bowl, Giants beat Philadelphia 14-1
Heinkel He 176 rocket plane flies for 1st time, at Peenemunde
"Brenda Starr, Reporter", 1st cartoon strip by a woman, Dale [Dalia] Messick, begins as a comic-book supplement to Chicago's Sunday Tribune
German troops begin the invasion of the undefended Channel Islands
US Fish and Wildlife Service forms
Ukrainian Nationalists (OUN) declare Ukraine independent
World War II: Operation Barbarossa - Germany captures Lviv, Ukraine
Col-general Von Paul's 6th Army enters Ukraine
U-boats sink and damage 146 allied ships this month (700,227 tons)
US bombs Celebes and Timor
US Mint in New Orleans ceases operation
Allies land on Vogelkop, New Guinea
French Cotentin Peninsula in allied hands
Universal strike against Nazi terror in Copenhagen
World War II: The Battle of Cherbourg ends with the fall of the strategically valuable port to American forces
17-day newspaper strike in NY begins
Cleveland Indians' future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcher Bob Lemon no-hits Detroit Tigers, 2-0
Last British troops leave Israel
Transistor as a substitute for valves announced (Bell Labs)
Dutch troops evacuate Jakarta
USGeneral MacArthurvisits front in South Korea, asks for US troops
NAACP begins attack on school segregation & discrimination
"Guiding Light" soap opera moves from radio to TV
Hussein Sirri Pasha forms Egyptian government
1st Chevrolet Corvette manufactured
Largest check: Internal US Treasury check at $4,176,969,623.57
United DC-7 & TWA collide over Grand Canyon killing 128
"No Chemise, Please" by Gerry Grenahan peaks at #24
Dutch government of Willem Drees ends obligatory dismissal of married teachers
During a game in Wrigley Field, 2 balls are in play at same time
Democratic Republic of the Congo (formerly Belgian Congo) declares independence from Belgium
US stops sugar import from Cuba
Buddy Rogers beats Pat O'Conner in Chicago, to become NWA champ
NASA Explorer Micrometeoroid research mission fails to reach Earth orbit
French Foreign Legion leaves Algeria
LA Dodgers' future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherSandy Koufaxno-hits New York Mets, 5-0; first of 4 career no-hitters thrown by Koufax
Premier Ben Khedda disbands Algerian Liberation Army fighters
Rwanda and Burundi become independent
Ciaculli massacre: A car bomb, intended for Mafia boss Salvatore Greco, kills seven police and military officers near Palermo
International Labour Organisation excludes South Africa from its two-day meeting because of its apartheid policies
Centaur 3 launch vehicle fails to make Earth orbit
Last UN troops leave Congo
England spin bowler Derek Underwood goes wicketless in his Test cricket debut v West Indies at Nottingham; captures 297 wickets in illustrious 86 Test career
Leopoldville, Congo, is renamed Kinshasa
Richard Helms promoted from deputy director to 8th director of US Central Intelligence Agency

Some attendees at the National Conference of Commissions on the Status of Women in Washington D.C. lay groundwork to establish a group to speak for women's rights - the National Organization of Women (NOW)
The Beatles land in Tokyo, Japan for what becomes their final concert tour there - they perform 5 shows at the Budokan Hall over 3 days, and collaborate on a painting while there
Vice Admiral William F. Raborn Jr., USN, ends term as 7th director of CIA
Former Congolese Prime Minister Moise Tsjombe's plane hijacked to Algeria
Philadelphia Phillies utility man Cookie Rojas pitches a scoreless 9th inning in 12-3 loss to SF Giants; Rojas had now played all 9 positions since arriving in MLB in 1963
Gaullists win French parliamentary election, 358 of 458 seats
In South Africa, General Laws Amendment Bill is passed; the Bill contains far-reaching provisions and restrictions affecting the administration of justice and the disclosure of evidence
Spain cedes Ifni to Morocco
Cincinnati's Riverfront Stadium opens as the visiting Atlanta Braves beat Reds, 8-2; demolished 2002
IBM announces its System/370 mainframe computer
Crew of Russian space mission Soyuz 11 found dead upon arrival on earth becoming the only people to die in space
One leap second is added to the UTC time system; also 1981, 1982, 1983, 1985
Ulster Defence Association (UDA) begin to organise its own 'no-go' areas (this is a response to the continuation of Republican 'no-go' areas and fears about concessions to the IRA)
"Burns & Schreiber Comedy Hour" TV Variety; debut on ABC
Biggest US tanker "Brooklyn" christened (230,000 ton)
Observers aboard Concorde jet observe 72-min solar eclipse
Canadian Open Golf (Peter Jackson Classic), Candiac GC: Carole Jo Skala wins by 3 from JoAnn Carner
Petty thief Peter Leonard sets fire to hide his burglary at a bowling alley, fires spreads next door to "Gulliver's" nightclub killing 24 (Port Chester, New York)
Soviet dancer Mikhail Baryshnikov defects to the West
Bundy victim Shelley Robertson disappears in Colorado
University of California reports galaxy 3C123 at 8 billion light years distance
John Walker of NZ sets record for 2000 meters, 4:51.4
US Railway Post Office final train run (NY to Washington, D.C.)
Yankee DH Cliff Johnson hit 3 consecutive HRs in Toronto
Giants'Willie McCoveybecomes 12th to hit 500 HRs
"Good Times" single released by Chic (Billboard Song of the Year 1979)
British sixpence demonetised after being in used since 1551 and 12 years after introduction of decimal currency
Zwelakhe Sisulu, President of the Black Media Workers Association of South Africa, is detained
New Jersey NHL franchise officially named Devils by fan balloting; legend of the Jersey Devil, a creature that allegedly inhabited the Pine Barrens of South Jersey
Orbiter Challenger (OV-099) rolled out at Palmdale
KBS Special Live Broadcast “Finding Dispersed Families” begins daily in Korea, documenting reunion attempts after the Cold War (later included in UNESCO's Memory of the World)
Failed coup by cocaine growers in Bolivia
Longest pro football game, LA Express beats Mich Panthers 27-21 in USFL playoffs, games lasts 93 minutes 33 seconds
39 remaining hostages from hijacked TWA Flight 847 are freed in Beirut
LA Dodger Pedro Gonzalez sets NL record of 15 HRs in June
Georgia sodomy law upheld by US Supreme Court (5-4)
Patrik Sjöberg of Sweden sets a new men's high jump world record 2.42m (7 ft 11 1⁄4 in) in Stockholm, Sweden
The Royal Canadian Mint introduces the $1 coin, known as the Loonie
Chicago agrees to build a new stadium so White Sox won't move to Florida
French archbishop Marcel Lefebvre is excommunicated by the Roman Catholic Church for consecrating traditionalist bishops
Sitcom "Sledge Hammer!" last airs on ABC-TV
NASA closes down tracking stations in Santiago, Chile & Guam

Spanish conquistadors are expelled from Tenochtitlan following an Aztec revolt against their rule underHernán Cortésduring "La Noche Triste" (the Night of Sadness). Many soldiers drown in the escape, and Aztec emperorMoctezuma IIdies in the struggle.
Augsburg Interim issued byCharles Vbecomes law - orders Lutherans to return to Catholic practices with some concessions
KingHenry IIof France is seriously injured in a jousting match against Gabriel de Montgomery
KingPhilip II of Spainmoves to El Escorial royal palace (dies there three months later)
Whig Lords questions princeWilliam IIIof Orange on Protestantism
Hungarian Parliament condemns EmperorCharles VI's Pragmatic Sanctions - to recognise his daughters as his heirs
British Commissioners of Longitude grant self-taught clockmakerJohn Harrison£500 after the successful trial of his first longitude clock
Famous debate onCharles Darwin's theory of evolution is held at Oxford University Museum, dominated by arguments between Thomas Henry Huxley and Bishop Samuel Wilberforce
French writerGustave Flaubertcompletes his novel "Salammbo"
Yosemite Grant Act signed by PresidentAbraham Lincolnbestowing 200,000 acres to the state of California, establishes America's first state-controlled park
27 year-old clerkAlbert Einsteinpublishes his theory of special relativity, the relationship between space and time in equation E=mc2
Conservative Australian Prime Minister George H. Reid is forced to resign andAlfred Deakinreturns to power on July 5
US Congress passes the Meat Inspection Act and the Pure Food and Drug Act; these laws owe much to the expose journalism of the period (Upton Sinclair's 'The Jungle' in particular)
At 41 years, 3 months future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherCy Youngbecomes oldest player to record a third career no-hitter as Boston Red Sox beat NY Highlanders, 8-0
Jack Johnsonfights Tony Ross to a no decision in 6 rounds at Duquesne Gardens, Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania to retain his heavyweight boxing title
Mahatma Gandhi's 1st arrest after campaigning for Indian rights in South Africa
J. B. M. Hertzogbecomes Primes Minister, head of a coalition government between the National and Labour Parties in South Africa
Adolf Hitlerstages a bloody purge of the Nazi party in the "Night of the Long Knives"
Ethiopian EmperorHaile Selassieasks the League of Nations for sanctions against Italy
Margaret Mitchell's novel "Gone With The Wind" is published
Allied forces underGeneral MacArthurbegin Operation Cartwheel (island-hopping) in the South West Pacific to try to destroy Japanese base at Rabaul
"Victor BorgeShow" last airs on NBC-TV
Variety TV show "TheJohnny CarsonShow" debuts on CBS TV
Vladimir Lenin's politics testament (1923) published in Moscow
NFL CommissionerPete Rozellegrants ownership of the Atlanta Falcons to Rankin Smith Sr., Executive VP of Life Insurance Company of Georgia; Rankin pays then-record $8.5 million
Robert Henry Lawrence, Jr.named 1st black astronaut
East German Communist Party leaderWalter Ulbrichtreceives the "Order of the October Revolution" for services to communism
Muhammad Aliretains world heavyweight boxing crown by beating Englishman Joe Bugner by unanimous points decision in a re-match in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
MarvelComics publish "Kiss" comic book feting rock group Kiss
US PresidentJimmy Cartercans B-1A bomber later "B-1's the B-52"
Future Baseball Hall of Fame outfielderLarry Dobybecomes only the second African-American manager in MLB with the Chicago White Sox
British punk rockerJohnny Rottenand British actressJoan Collinsappear together on BBC TV's "Juke Box Jury"
West German ChancellorHelmut Schmidtvisits Moscow
China's Communist Party condemns lateMao Zedong's policy
Federal Equal Rights Amendment fails 3 states short of ratification
Pierre Trudeauofficially steps down as Prime Minister of Canada after serving two separate terms for a total of 15 years
Brooklyn, New York dedicates a bus depot honoringJackie Gleasonand his role as bus driver Ralph Kramden in "The Honeymooners"

"Great Balls of Fire!" a quasi-biographical drama film starringDennis Quaidas rockabilly pioneerJerry Lee Lewispremieres; Lewis hates the film and the book from which it is sourced, but praises Quaid's performance
Gelderland goes on Gregorian calendar (tomorrow is 12/7/1700)

A giant fireball, most likely caused by the air burst of a large meteoroid or comet, flattens 80 million trees near the Stony Tunguska River in Yeniseysk Governorate, Russia, in the largest impact event in recorded history
British GeneralDouglas Haigreports "The men are in splendid spirits" the day before the Battle of the Somme begins
